name.description: Gets or sets the command name. This name is used as the command identifier in the CLI. For root commands, this name may be used as the application name.

description.description: Gets or sets the command description. This text appears in help screens when users request help for this command.

options.description: Gets or sets the options specific to this command. Options are named parameters that can be specified with a prefix (e.g., --verbose, -v).

arguments.description: Gets or sets the positional arguments of this command. Arguments are ordered parameters that don't have a name prefix.

commands.description: Gets or sets the nested subcommands. Subcommands allow creating hierarchical command structures (e.g., git commit, git push).

validators.description: Gets or sets the command-level validators. Validators are C# code snippets that run after all options and arguments are parsed.

extensions.description: Gets or sets the command-level extensions. Extensions are custom methods that can be called on the command instance.

name.description: Gets or sets the option name. This is the primary identifier used to generate the long form (e.g., --name).

type.description: Gets or sets the .NET type name for the option value. Supported built-in types: string, bool, int, long, double, float, decimal, DateTime, FileInfo, DirectoryInfo, string[], int[]. Custom types will be wrapped in ComplexOption<T>.

description.description: Gets or sets the option description. This text appears in help screens when users request help for this option.

default.description: Gets or sets the default value for the option. The value is parsed according to the specified type.

required.description: Gets or sets whether this option is required. Required options must be provided on the command line.

aliases.description: Gets or sets the alternative names/short forms for this option. Aliases provide alternative ways to specify the same option.

validators.description: Gets or sets the option-level validators. Validators are C# code snippets that validate the option value after parsing.

recursive.description: Gets or sets whether this option should recursively process directories. When true, directory paths are processed recursively.

allowMultipleArgumentsPerToken.description: Gets or sets whether multiple arguments can be specified per token. When true, values can be comma-separated or space-separated within a single token.

acceptExistingOnly.description: Gets or sets whether the option value must point to an existing file or directory. When true, the path must exist on the filesystem.

acceptLegalFileNamesOnly.description: Gets or sets whether the option value must be a legal file name. When true, the value must not contain invalid characters for file names.

acceptOnlyFromAmong.description: Gets or sets the list of allowed values for this option. The option value must be one of the strings in this list.

arity.description:
Gets or sets the arity (number of values expected) for this option.

Valid values:
  • "1" - Exactly one value (default)
  • "?" - Zero or one value
  • "*" - Zero or more values
  • "+" - One or more values
  • "0..1" - Zero or one value
  • "1..*" - One or more values
  • "0..*" - Zero or more values
  • "2" - Exactly two values
  • "2..5" - Between 2 and 5 values
  • "2..*" - Two or more values

Examples:
  arity: "1"        # Exactly one value
  arity: "?"        # Optional, at most one
  arity: "*"        # Any number, including zero
  arity: "+"        # At least one
  arity: "2..5"     # Between 2 and 5 values
  arity: "0..1"     # Zero or one
  arity: "1..*"     # One or more
  arity: "0..*"     # Zero or more

extensions.description: Gets or sets the option-level extensions. Extensions are custom methods that can be called on the option instance.

code.description: The C# code that implements the validation. The code should use a parameter named 'result' of the appropriate type. To report an error, set result.ErrorMessage.

name.description: Gets or sets the name of the extension method to call. The method must be an extension method defined in a static class that is in scope.

parameters.description: Gets or sets the parameters to pass to the extension method. Parameters are written as literals in the generated code.

name.description: Gets or sets the argument name. This is used as the identifier in help text and generated code.

type.description: Gets or sets the .NET type name for the argument value. Supported built-in types: string, bool, int, long, double, float, decimal, DateTime, FileInfo, DirectoryInfo, string[], int[]. Custom types will be wrapped in ComplexArgument<T>.

description.description: Gets or sets the argument description. This text appears in help screens.

default.description: Gets or sets the default value for the argument. The value is parsed according to the specified type. Arguments with default values become optional.

arity.description:
Gets or sets the arity (number of values expected) for this argument.

Valid values:
  • "1" - Exactly one value (default)
  • "?" - Zero or one value
  • "*" - Zero or more values
  • "+" - One or more values
  • "0..1" - Zero or one value
  • "1..*" - One or more values
  • "0..*" - Zero or more values
  • "2" - Exactly two values
  • "2..5" - Between 2 and 5 values
  • "2..*" - Two or more values

Examples:
  arity: "1"        # Exactly one value
  arity: "?"        # Optional, at most one
  arity: "*"        # Any number, including zero
  arity: "+"        # At least one
  arity: "2..5"     # Between 2 and 5 values
  arity: "0..1"     # Zero or one
  arity: "1..*"     # One or more
  arity: "0..*"     # Zero or more

validators.description: Gets or sets the argument-level validators. Validators are C# code snippets that validate the argument value after parsing.

acceptExistingOnly.description: For file/directory arguments: only accept paths that already exist.

acceptLegalFileNamesOnly.description: For file/directory arguments: only accept valid file/directory names (no invalid characters, etc.).

acceptOnlyFromAmong.description: For arguments that should only accept values from a predefined set (e.g., "red", "green", "blue").

allowMultipleArgumentsPerToken.description: For arguments that can accept multiple values in a single token (e.g., "arg1,arg2,arg3").

extensions.description: Gets or sets the argument-level extensions. Extensions are custom methods that can be called on the argument instance.
